new_hardware_experiment.reason: The current repository has no trajectory execution bridge from the planned joint trajectory to an SR5 controller.

current_repo_evidence.finding: ROS adapter exposes one Trigger service that calls LevelConstrainedPlanner.plan and writes result files; AST audit found create_service=1, create_publisher=0, create_client=0, publish=0, send_goal=0.
The adapter guide states that motion, RViz, vision, scanner, gripper, and state-machine nodes are outside this adapter.
Runtime requests are validation jobs, not direct execution commands.

old_project_evidence.finding: The old-project safety audit passes default safety guards, but the smoke report status is not_executed_template_and_safety_audit_only and single_trajectory_smoke.executed is false.
old_project_evidence.paper_use: May be cited only as prior validation or engineering background if the old project is explicitly referenced; do not count it as a first-party result of this repository.

paper_implication.allowed_claim: The same SR5 platform had prior safety-guarded validation in earlier work.
disallowed_claims: This paper executed a new 20/20 or 18/20 real-robot trial.
This repository contains a complete SR5 trajectory execution bridge.
Learned diffusion candidates were executed on the real robot.
required_before_new_hardware: Implement an explicit low-speed trajectory execution bridge or action client.
Add a human-confirmed safety checklist covering speed limits, emergency stop, workspace limits, collision pre-stop, and personnel separation.
Run a single manually selected plan-only trajectory through simulation/preview before hardware.
Record controller feedback, final pose tolerance, full-path level tolerance, collision status, and joint tracking error.
